raw_response:
I agree with the initial assessment that pigmented nevi are most commonly found in the buccal mucosa (Option C). One additional point supporting this is the fact that the buccal mucosa has fewer melanocytes compared to other oral sites like the hard palate, which might contribute to higher incidences of these benign skin lesions due to less inherent pigmentary protection. 

Therefore, my final answer remains: C (Buccal Mucosa).
